mapping.a_element: isolated quantum many-body system relaxing to a universal stationary state (thermalization)
mapping.b_element: quantum gas undergoing a phase transition to a self-organized supersolid phase

mapping.a_element: eigenstate thermalization scenario (individual eigenstates encoding thermal averages)
mapping.b_element: Dicke Hamiltonian describing the phase transition and spontaneous symmetry breaking

mapping.a_element: microcanonical energy window where all eigenstates yield the same thermal averages
mapping.b_element: critical boundary of the phase transition mapped in agreement with the Dicke model

brief_justification: While both papers address quantum many-body systems, Paper A focuses on thermalization via eigenstate properties in isolated systems, whereas Paper B describes a quantum phase transition driven by long-ranged interactions and symmetry breaking in an open system. The underlying mechanisms—eigenstate thermalization vs. Dicke model phase transition—are distinct, despite superficial overlap in quantum many-body dynamics.
